page: Amazon Web Services x1e.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz and includes a 45 MB L3 cache. This configuration is paired with 122.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 30.5 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 120 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the instance exhibits limited computational and memory throughput performance relative to modern standards. In stress-ng and Geekbench testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 10% of evaluated servers. Memory bandwidth benchmarks also reflect constraints, with small-block cached reads scoring in the bottom 25% and large-block uncached reads and writes falling into the bottom 10%. Database operations, such as Redis SET and PassMark database tests, similarly rank in the bottom 10%, indicating that the platform's strength lies in its capacity rather than its processing speed.

The primary tradeoff of the x1e.xlarge is its high memory capacity relative to its modest, older-generation CPU performance. While it offers high resource density for memory-bound tasks, its low computational efficiency and limited network bandwidth make it less suitable for CPU-intensive workloads or high-throughput web serving. Qualitatively, this instance represents a specialized hardware class where the cost is aligned with the massive RAM allocation rather than raw processing power. It is suited for workloads requiring a large memory footprint but low CPU utilization, such as small-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, or enterprise applications with high RAM demands.
description: Amazon Web Services x1e.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for workloads requiring a large memory footprint relative to compute resources. Powered by an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor running at 2.3 GHz, it provides 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores) and 122.0 GB of RAM, alongside a 120 GB NVMe SSD and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark evaluations place its CPU and memory bandwidth performance in the lower tiers, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and database operations scoring in the bottom 10% to 25%. This indicates a clear tradeoff: the instance offers high memory density but limited processing speed. Consequently, the x1e.xlarge is suited for memory-intensive applications with low CPU demands, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and specialized enterprise software.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x1e.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 122.0 GB of RAM, and a 120 GB NVMe SSD, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low CPU utilization.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x1e.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 122.0 GB of RAM, and a 120 GB NVMe SSD for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring an exceptionally high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
122.0 GB of system memory providing 30.5 GB per core
Suited for memory-intensive workloads with low CPU demands
